semanticCore: Whistling, blowing with the mouth, sound of a bird, or empty whistling wind

keyDerivations.meaning: Whistling, high-pitched mouth whistling, or bird whistling sounds

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَمَا كَانَ صَلَاتُهُمْ عِنْدَ الْبَيْتِ إِلَّا مُكَاءً وَتَصْدِيَةً (8:35): And their prayer at the House was nothing but whistling and hand-clapping (distracting pagan rituals).
discardedNoise: Makkah (city of Mecca) - unrelated proper noun M-K-K.
summaryEn: The root refers to whistling sounds (Mukaan), condemning the pagan rituals of Quraysh who whistled and clapped around the Kaaba to mock and disrupt Islamic prayers.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى الصفير بالشبك باليد والنفخ بالفم كصفير الطائر (مكو)، مستنكراً تحول عبادة مشركي قريش عند الكعبة إلى مجرد صفير (مكاء) وتصفيق.
rawText: مُكَّآءٌ The bird so called, because of its colour: see أَخْرَجُ.
